name: JClaw
//: Read by VS Code Dev Containers, GitHub Codespaces, Cursor, JetBrains Gateway, and any other devcontainer-spec-compliant IDE. Build from the local Dockerfile so the image carries a known-good toolchain (JDK 25, Node 20, corepack, Play 1.x, Python 3, tesseract-ocr) — no reliance on a generic base image plus features that may drift in version. The Dockerfile installs every prereq jclaw.sh setup's check_prereqs validates, so postCreateCommand never fails on a missing dep at this layer.
//postCreateCommand: Runs the same setup script a human would invoke after a fresh clone, AFTER the image build finishes and the workspace is bind-mounted. Wires git hooks, runs corepack install + pnpm install, validates prereqs (which all pass thanks to the Dockerfile), and adds the github remote. Setup is idempotent, so re-running on container rebuild is harmless.
//forwardPorts: Backend on 9000, Nuxt dev server on 3000 — match the defaults in jclaw.sh's BACKEND_PORT / FRONTEND_PORT.
//customizations: VS Code / Cursor extensions that match the project's stack. Vue + TypeScript for the frontend; Java extension pack for the backend; ESLint + Stylelint for the lint-staged pre-commit hook.
